title: Query
description: Schema for query JSON files that define queries for classes. Query files are located alongside their class file with '.queries.json' extension (e.g., 'order_management/order.queries.json' for class 'order_management/order.class.json'). Queries represent read-only operations that return information without modifying state. The file contains a map of query keys to query definitions. If validation fails, verify 'name' is present and non-empty for each query.

name.description: The display name of the query (e.g., 'Get Order Total', 'Find Pending Orders', 'Calculate Shipping Cost'). This is the human-readable name shown in documentation. Use verb phrases that describe what information the query returns. Must be non-empty. Only letters (A-Z, a-z), digits (0-9), spaces, hyphens, and underscores are allowed. If validation fails: remove any characters that are not in this set.

details.description: Optional detailed description of the query. Describe what information the query returns, any parameters it requires, and performance considerations. This helps implementers understand the query's purpose and expected behavior.

parameters.description: Parameters for the query. Each parameter has a name and optional data type rules.

data_type_rules.description: Data type rules for the parameter value.

requires.description: Preconditions that must be true before the query can execute. Each item is a logic specification with a description and optional formal notation. Specifications must not contain primed values. Items may also be 'let' bindings that define local variables for use by subsequent items.

type.description: Required. The logic kind. Use 'assessment' for boolean preconditions, 'let' for local variable definitions.

description.description: A precondition statement. Describe what must be true for this query to execute correctly.

target.description: Required for 'let' type: the local variable name. Must be omitted or empty for 'assessment' type.

target_type_spec.description: Optional TLA+ type declaration for the target (e.g., 'Int', 'STRING'). Only meaningful for 'let' type.

notation.description: Required. The formal notation system. Currently the only supported value is 'tla_plus'. Must always be present. If validation fails: ensure this is exactly 'tla_plus'.

specification.description: Optional formal specification in the given notation. Must not contain primed values.

guarantees.description: Postconditions describing what the query returns. Each guarantee defines one output via its 'target' field (the output identifier name). The target separates the output name so the specification contains only the value expression with no primed values. Each output identifier can only appear once per query. Items may also be 'let' bindings that define local variables for use by subsequent items.

type.description: Required. The logic kind. Use 'query' for named return values, 'let' for local variable definitions.

description.description: A postcondition statement describing the query result.

target.description: Required. For 'query': the output identifier name (e.g., 'result', 'totalAmount'). For 'let': the local variable name.

target_type_spec.description: Optional TLA+ type declaration for the target (e.g., 'Int', 'STRING'). Only meaningful for 'let' type.

notation.description: Required. The formal notation system. Currently the only supported value is 'tla_plus'. Must always be present. If validation fails: ensure this is exactly 'tla_plus'.

specification.description: Optional formal specification in the given notation. This must be only the value expression with no primed values. The output identifier is captured by the 'target' field.
